hypothesis: Integrating 3D pharmacophore descriptors that explicitly encode inter-feature distances and angles into the reinforcement-learning reward will increase the proportion of generated compounds achieving in vitro ERα inhibitory IC50 ≤ 1 μM by at least two-fold compared to generation using only 2D CATS-based pharmacophore rewards, while preserving a mean structural Tanimoto similarity to the reference set below 0.4.
